memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Alice Grigle or Grigges
memoryArtifacts.description:
Braintree, Essex, widow.  Written 22 Feb 1577; proved 16 Feb 1584.

My body to be buried in the parish church or churchyard of Branktree.  

I give and bequeath unto John Motte my son one field or cro …

Braintree, Essex, England, yeoman.  18 Oct 1575; proved 10 Jun 1577

To be buried in the church or churchyard there.  Lands and tenements in Boeking.  Wife Alice to have lands, tenements, etc. in Brai …

zh.St Dunstan and All Saints, London, Engla: 英國英格蘭倫敦韋斯特 漢姆哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Mead於1525年出生在英國艾可布萊崔時，她的父親Simon Thomas Meade38歲，母親Anne Moody25歲。她於1540年與John Thomas Mott在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1584年2月在英國英格蘭倫敦韋斯特 漢姆哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇享年59歲。

zh-hans.St Dunstan and All Saints, London, Engla: 英国英格兰倫敦韋斯特 漢姆哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Mead于1525年出生在英国艾可布萊崔时，她的父亲Simon Thomas Meade38岁，母亲Anne Moody25岁。她于1540年与John Thomas Mott在英国结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1584年2月在英国英格兰倫敦韋斯特 漢姆哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mead
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Middle English mede ‘meadow’ (Old English mǣd), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places so named, such as Meads in Harford (Devon).
perhaps from Middle English mede ‘mead, alcoholic drink of fermented honey and water’, possibly denoting one who was fond of the drink or who made or sold mead. Compare Meader .

variants.name: Meadow
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Middle English medewe, medu (Old English mǣdwe, dative of mǣd). Compare Mead 1.

variants.name: Meads
meanings: English: variant of Mead , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Meade
meanings: Irish: habitational name from Midheach, the Gaelic name of County Meath in Ireland. Its Anglicized forms are now indistinguishable from those of the English name Mead .

variants.name: Mather
meanings: occupational name for a mower or reaper of grass or hay, Old English mǣthere. Compare Mead , Mower . Hay was formerly of great importance, not only as feed for animals in winter but also for bedding.
variant of Mader , reflecting an alternative pronunciation influenced by Old Norse mathra ‘madder (plant)’.

variants.name: Meader
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English meder ‘maker or seller of mead’. Compare Mead 2.

variants.name: Medd
meanings: variant of   Mead   with shortening of the vowel.
possibly from Middle English Medde, a rare personal name of obscure origin.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Mead was born in 1525, in Braintree, Essex, England, her father, Simon Thomas Meade, was 38 and her mother, Anne Moody, was 25. She married John Thomas Mott in 1540, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in February 1584, in All Saints Church, West Ham, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

Elle a épousé John Thomas Mott en 1540, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en février 1584, à All Saints Church, West Ham, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.

ko.St Dunstan and All Saints, London, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트 함 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Mead(이)가 1525년 영국 에세스 브레이트리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon Thomas Meade(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Moody(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1540년 영국에서 John Thomas Mott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1584년 2월 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트 함 앨 세인츠 c후르치에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com John Thomas Mott em 1540, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.St Dunstan and All Saints, London, Engla: алл сеинц чурч, вест хам, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Mead родилась в 1525 г. в местности бреинтри, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Simon Thomas Meade было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Moody, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Thomas Mott в 1540 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла февраль 1584 г. в местности алл сеинц чурч, вест хам, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет.
